Event: He plans manipulative, self-serving ways to get with Britta despite her lack of interest
Explanation: He seeks sexual/emotional gain while disregarding her wishes and autonomy.

Event: He weasels his way into being alone with Britta during her existential crisis
Explanation: He maneuvers the situation to isolate a vulnerable person for his own prospective benefit.

Event: He has a change of heart and decides not to take advantage of vulnerable Britta
Explanation: He foregoes a potential exploitative gain to protect her well-being and his own conscience/relationship.

Event: He momentarily regrets not taking advantage after Britta says she would have consented
Explanation: He emotionally punishes himself for choosing the ethical path despite no one being harmed.

Event: He drinks excessively (likes his booze a little too much)
Explanation: His alcoholism harms his own functioning while offering no clear benefit to others.

Event: He as anthropology teacher refuses to engage with the subject or teach usefully
Explanation: He keeps his job/comfort while depriving students of proper education.

Event: He does not buy the anthropology textbook because it’s too expensive
Explanation: He saves his own money and effort while undermining course quality for students.

Event: He spends entire weeks playing YouTube videos instead of teaching
Explanation: He makes his own workload easier while denying students genuine instruction.

Event: He improvises an anesthesiology class because a pretty student got the room wrong
Explanation: He indulges a whim without clear gain and wastes students’ time with irrelevant content.

Event: He sets the anthropology final exam as an excuse for everyone to drink
Explanation: He engineers a party-like exam for his own enjoyment at the cost of academic integrity.

Event: He helps Professor Duncan get out of a legal scrape involving driving
Explanation: He uses his skills to save Duncan from legal trouble and likely builds goodwill.

Event: He treats Jeff more nicely than others because Jeff once helped him
Explanation: He repays a favor, benefiting their relationship without harming others.

Event: He attempts to get Jeff to give up his manipulative ways in the pilot
Explanation: He tries to guide Jeff toward more honest behavior, which can benefit both of them.

Event: He conducts a cruel psychological study with Annie helping him
Explanation: He pursues professional/academic interests via an experiment framed as cruel to participants.

Event: He reveals lonely unprocessed childhood trauma when pressed or drunk
Explanation: He exposes his own pain under pressure, which can help others understand him more than it helps himself.

Event: He plays piano well for the Glee Club
Explanation: He uses his talent in a group activity, giving enjoyment to others and social value to himself.

Event: He establishes “The Duncan Principle” experiment
Explanation: He seeks academic recognition through a manipulative design that burdens participants.

Event: Abed waits twenty-six hours for the meeting Duncan promised (backfiring on Duncan)
Explanation: Abed gains data/validation at the cost of Duncan becoming victim of his own exploitative setup.

Event: He runs ethically questionable experiments and lures patients into therapy to hit on them and get publication material
Explanation: He uses clients for sexual and professional gain while risking their well-being and trust.

Event: He sets the anthro final as a drinking excuse and orders Annie bullied down when she questions fairness
Explanation: He protects his sham class and party plan by socially suppressing a conscientious student.

Event: He hides from the Dean that anthropology has been a complete dodge of a class
Explanation: He conceals his negligence to avoid consequences, worsening institutional and student outcomes.

Event: The Dean walks in during Duncan’s celebratory toast and scuttles his plans
Explanation: The Dean accidentally enforces accountability, curbing Duncan’s misuse of the exam for drinking.

Event: Abed repeatedly turns Duncan’s psychoanalysis back on him
Explanation: Abed protects himself and gains insight while destabilizing Duncan’s authority and comfort.

Event: He advises Jeff in the pilot to earn his degree honorably
Explanation: He encourages genuine effort, potentially reforming Jeff and reflecting well on himself.

Event: He performs a harmless secret test of character on Jeff
Explanation: He mildly manipulates Jeff in order to nudge him toward better behavior without real harm.

Event: He throws a full-blown temper tantrum when his experiment backfires
Explanation: His meltdown emotionally harms himself and undermines his standing while helping no one.

Event: He decides not to take advantage of Britta in Season 5, drives her home, and gives her a pep talk
Explanation: He actively supports her emotionally and safely, also improving his own character and relationships.

Event: He chooses to remain on The Daily Show rather than become a regular on Community
Explanation: He prioritizes his own career stability over the show’s original casting plans.
